steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence says era names uniformly describe evolutionary phases. The second sentence introduces period names and says they have diverse origins. Uniformity versus diversity is a direct contrast — the transition must signal opposition.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘therefore,’: signals a conclusion or consequence. The second sentence is not a result of the first. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘in turn,’: signals sequence or a reciprocal action. No sequence is implied here. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘on the other hand,’: directly signals contrast between two opposing ideas — eras follow one pattern, periods follow another. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘furthermore,’: signals addition, not opposition. It would suggest the two ideas reinforce each other rather than contrast. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
